s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 brake pads with the anti-squeal shims.
Remove the 2 anti-squeal shims and the 2 pad wear indicator plates from each pad.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 REAR DISC BRAKE PAD SUPPORT PLATE

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 1 rear disc brake pad support plate from the rear disc brake cylinder mounting.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 REAR DISC BRAKE PAD SUPPORT PLATE

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 2 rear disc brake pad support plate from the rear disc brake cylinder mounting.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE CYLINDER MOUNTING

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and the rear disc brake cylinder mounting.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE CYLINDER SLIDE PIN

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 pins (upper and lower) from the rear brake cylinder mounting.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE BUSHING DUST BOOT

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 rear disc brake bushing dust boots from the rear disc brake cylinder mounting.
Using a screwdriver, pry out the set ring and cylinder boot.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the brake piston and cylinder.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Prepare a wooden plate to hold the brake pistons.
Place the wooden plate between the brake piston and the rear disc brake cylinder sub-assembly.
Use compressed air to remove the piston from the disc brake cylinder assembly.

atten2.ptxt: Do not place your fingers in front of the brake piston when using compressed air.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to spatter the brake fluid.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the piston seal from the rear disc brake cylinder sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the inner cylinder and cylinder groove.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE BLEEDER PLUG CAP

s2.ptxt: Remove the bleeder plug cap from the rear disc brake bleeder plug.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE BLEEDER PLUG

s2.ptxt: Remove the rear disc brake bleeder plug from the rear disc brake cylinder sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE SHOE ADJUSTING HOLE PLUG

s2.ptxt: Remove the parking brake shoe adjusting hole plug.
Put matchmarks on the rear disc and the axle hub.
Release the parking brake and remove the rear disc.

atten4.ptxt: If the disc cannot be removed easily, turn and press firmly the shoe adjuster until the wheel comes free.
